novel_features.name: Batch generate from a prompt file
novel_features.description: Generate many images from a file of prompts in one quota-aware run.
novel_features.rationale: The web UI is strictly one-prompt-at-a-time; this loops the generate endpoint over a prompt/CSV/JSONL file, caps concurrency to the account limit, and refuses to exceed the undocumented ~400/day cap where even failed generations count.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user has more than one prompt or a spreadsheet of variations; it is the headline workflow no manual web session can match.

novel_features.name: Generate, wait, and download in one shot
novel_features.description: Submit a generation, block until it renders, and save the images to disk.
novel_features.rationale: Completion is pushed over a Pusher WebSocket the web UI consumes and scripts cannot see; --wait polls the design list until status flips processing->private, then --download pulls the real CDN images.
novel_features.example: artistly-pp-cli generate "a watercolor fox in a misty forest" --wait --download ./out --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to make image generation a single blocking step in a script or agent flow instead of a manual click-and-babysit loop.

novel_features.name: Offline prompt search over your archive
novel_features.description: Full-text search your own generation history by prompt, style, folder, or date — entirely offline.
novel_features.rationale: Artistly offers no search over your own designs; sync mirrors /fetch-personal-designs into local SQLite and search runs FTS over prompts, tags, checkpoint, and dimensions with no API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find an old design's exact prompt/seed to reuse, instead of scrolling the web gallery.

novel_features.name: Reproduce or remix a past design
novel_features.description: Resubmit a past design's exact settings (prompt, style, dimensions, seed) with optional overrides.
novel_features.rationale: Reads a prior design's saved params from the local mirror and resubmits via the generate endpoint; the web UI has no 'make more like this' that reuses the original seed and settings.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user liked a previous result and wants variations or a re-run with one tweak.

novel_features.name: Generation settings presets
novel_features.description: Save a named bundle of generation settings (style, dimensions, aspect ratio, negative prompt, quality) and replay it.
novel_features.rationale: Artistly has no preset feature; this stores settings in local config and applies them across generate/batch with --preset, guaranteeing brand-consistent output with zero re-typing and zero quota cost.
novel_features.example: artistly-pp-cli preset save house-style --style watercolor --aspect-ratio 1:1 --quality highQuality
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user reuses one 'house style' across many prompts or campaigns.

novel_features.name: Bulk export by query
novel_features.description: Download all images from designs matching a query into a directory tree, with templated filenames — no generation.
novel_features.rationale: Selects designs from the local mirror by folder/date/prompt-match and downloads every CDN image with filename templating; the web UI downloads one asset at a time and never touches quota here.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to assemble a deliverable folder of already-generated assets without re-generating or clicking through downloads.

novel_features.name: Quota preflight / batch budget check
novel_features.description: Report remaining daily generations and whether a planned batch fits under the cap before you start.
novel_features.rationale: Does the arithmetic the web UI will not: reads the concurrent limit and today's count from page props and tells you if prompts x quantity fits under the ~400/day cap (where failures also count) before burning it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before a large batch so a quota wall does not silently truncate a run mid-way.

narrative.headline: The first CLI for Artistly — scriptable AI image generation, batch runs, and a searchable local archive the web app has no way to offer.
narrative.value_prop: Artistly (app.artistly.ai) is a login-only web app with no public API. This CLI authenticates with your browser session and turns the one-prompt-at-a-time web flow into something an agent or a Makefile can drive: batch-generate from a prompt file, block until images render and download them, search your whole generation history offline, and replay saved style presets — all while respecting the undocumented ~400/day generation cap.
narrative.auth_narrative: Artistly has no API keys. Authentication is your logged-in browser session: run `artistly-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import the `artistly_session` cookie and CSRF token from Chrome (be logged in to app.artistly.ai first). Generation and other write commands send the CSRF token and Inertia headers automatically.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI is wired up before authenticating.
After auth login --chrome: verify the session works and shows remaining daily generations.
Pull your most recent designs to confirm the session reads real data.

quickstart.command: artistly-pp-cli generate "a watercolor fox in a misty forest" --wait --download ./out
quickstart.comment: Generate one image, wait for it to render, and save it.

troubleshoots.symptom: Commands return 'not authenticated' or redirect to login
troubleshoots.fix: Run `artistly-pp-cli auth login --chrome` while logged in to app.artistly.ai in Chrome; the session cookie expires periodically.
Artistly may be under load; re-run `artistly-pp-cli designs list` to check status, or raise the wait timeout with `--wait-timeout`.
You likely hit the ~400/day cap (failed generations count too). Run `artistly-pp-cli quota` to see remaining budget before retrying.
Run `artistly-pp-cli sync` first to mirror your designs into the local store; search is offline-only.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need to drive Artistly image generation from a script or agent: batch-generating many prompt variations, blocking until images render and saving them to disk, searching or re-running past generations, or bulk-exporting finished assets into a deliverable folder. It is the right choice whenever the manual one-prompt web flow is too slow or not scriptable.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for outpaint or inpaint — not implemented in v1 (upscale and background removal ARE supported via 'edit').
Do not use this CLI for billing, subscription, or account-management changes — use the Artistly website.
Do not use this CLI to bypass Artistly's generation limits — it respects the daily cap rather than evading it.

recipes.title: Batch a spreadsheet of prompts and download finals
recipes.explanation: Reads each row as a prompt (with optional per-row overrides), applies a saved preset, waits for each to render, and saves the images — the Sunday-afternoon merch workflow in one command.

recipes.title: Find and re-run a past design
recipes.explanation: Search the local mirror for the old design, then resubmit its exact settings for more variations. The --agent --select pair narrows the verbose design records to just the fields you need.

recipes.title: Bulk-export a client's assets without re-generating
recipes.explanation: Downloads every finished image whose prompt matches, into a folder with readable filenames — zero quota cost.

recipes.title: Enhance a prompt before generating
recipes.command: artistly-pp-cli prompt enhance "a fox in a forest"
recipes.explanation: Expand a short prompt into a rich, detailed one (no quota cost), then feed it to generate. Use prompt extract <design-id|image-url> for the reverse (image-to-prompt).

recipes.title: Upscale or remove the background of a design
recipes.explanation: Run editor image-to-image tools on an existing design (by id/uuid), an image URL, or a local file. Use 'edit bg-remove' for background removal. Both auto-authenticate via your app session.
